headline: Home Depot adds tech and infrastructure to fix customer friction
summary: Amazon popularized the idea of paid membership for unlimited two-day delivery through Prime. That created a new delivery table stakes where consumers expected two-day delivery, and aside from paying for Amazon Prime, they generally have not been willing to pay for that privilege. headline: How Avis Stock Made the Dow Jones Transportation Average One of the Year’s Biggest Gainers
summary: Avis stock was up sevenfold through Monday to $609 a share, and its advance accounted for 52% of the index’s gain to a record level of 23,332.  Avis shares have been propelled higher in the past month by a giant short squeeze as two investors, SRS Investment Management and Pentwater Capital Management, control over 100% of Avis shares, including stock ownership and total return swaps that are equivalent to stock.  Avis shares are up another 13% to $686 Tuesday and that is lifting the Dow Jones transports by 2%.

Avis shares are up another 13% to $686 Tuesday and that is lifting the Dow Jones transports by 2%.

headline: Trump Encourages Companies Not to Seek Tariff Refunds

headline: New Ownership of 25-Year-Old PostalAnnex in Tigard, Oregon Brings Fresh Energy to the Business--Serving the Community with Shipping and Office Services
summary: Annex Brands, Inc., a leading franchisor in the packing, shipping, and office services industries, is pleased to announce the successful transfer of ownership of an existing PostalAnnex location in Tigard, OR to new owners, Tom Truong and Nhu-Linh Tran. This transition to new ownership ensures that the community will benefit from the essential shipping and office services provided by this PostalAnnex location for the last 25 years.

headline: Americans Paid Trump's Tariff: Corporations Now Sit On $166 Billion Refund
summary: CBP opened the refund portal Monday. Only importers can file — leaving U.S. households, who absorbed 86% of the tariff passthrough, shut out of the process.

raw.headline: Americans Paid Trump's Tariff: Corporations Now Sit On $166 Billion Refund
raw.summary: CBP opened the refund portal Monday. Only importers can file — leaving U.S. households, who absorbed 86% of the tariff passthrough, shut out of the process.

headline: FedEx, UPS and DHL detail tariff refund approach for customers
summary: The parcel carriers say they will return duties to parties that originally bore the cost for invalidated levies once they receive refunds from Customs and Border Protection.
